---
title: "rkagi Quickstart Guide"
author: "Rainer Krug"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
---

> A minimal, ropensci-style quickstart for using **rkagi** with the Kagi APIs
> (Search, Enrich, and the Universal Summarizer), featuring environment-based
> authentication, S3 results, and helper utilities.

## Installation

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_local(upgrade = "never")  # from your package root
# or: remotes::install_github("ropensci/rkagi")
```

## Authentication

The client reads your API key from the environment by default. Add the following
to your `~/.Renviron` and restart R:

```
KAGI_API_KEY=your_token_here
```

Alternatively, you can pass a **lazy** key-resolver (recommended with keyring):

```r
conn <- new_kagi_connection(
  endpoint = "search",
  api_key  = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi")
)
```

Keys are resolved **only at request time** via `resolve_api_key()`.

## Package Concepts

- **Connections**: `kagi_connection(base_url, endpoint, api_key)`
- **Requests**: small S3 objects (e.g. `kagi_search`, `summarizer_request`, `enrich_request`)
- **Results**: S3 objects with parsed JSON + metadata (e.g. `kagi_search_results`)
- **Helpers**: `build_kagi_q()` to compose advanced queries; `open_kagi_query()` to inspect them in a browser.

## Build a Precise Query

Use structured operators (`filetype:`, `site:`, `inurl:`, `intitle:`) — vectors are supported
and combined where appropriate (OR for `filetype` and `site`, AND for `inurl`/`intitle`).

```r
library(rkagi)

q <- build_kagi_q(
  query    = 'biodiversity "annual report"',
  filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),
  site     = c("example.com", "gov"),
  inurl    = c("2024", "report"),
  intitle  = "summary"
)

q
open_kagi_query(q)  # opens https://kagi.com/search?q=...
```

## Search API (closed beta)

Create a connection and a prepared search, then perform it. Results are typed S3
objects that print nicely and provide helpers to extract hits, related queries, and metadata.

```r
conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search")  # api_key resolved lazily

s <- new_kagi_search(conn, q = q, limit = 5)
s                                # prints the request

res <- kagi_perform(s, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))  # optional: save raw JSON
res                               # prints summary (hits, related, timing)

hits    <- kagi_hits(res)         # tibble of t == 0 results
related <- kagi_related(res)      # character vector of t == 1 related queries
meta    <- kagi_meta(res)         # list of { id, node, ms, api_balance }
```

### One-liner

```r
res <- kagi_search_once(q = q)
kagi_hits(res)
```

## Enrich API

The Enrich endpoint returns higher-signal results from specialized indices.
The workflow mirrors search: request → perform → helpers.

```r
conn_e <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "enrich")  # or 'enrich/web' based on your implementation
req_e  <- new_enrich_request(conn_e, q = q)         # enrich parameters as defined by your package
res_e  <- enrich_perform(req_e, path = tempfile(fileext = '.json'))

res_e                        # print summary
enrich_items <- enrich_extract(res_e)  # your helper to get a tibble (if provided)
```

> Tip: If your package exposes multiple enrich flavors (e.g., `/enrich/web`),
> consider mirrors of `new_enrich_request()` or a `type` argument.

## Universal Summarizer

Summarize a URL (GET) or raw text (POST). The call returns S3 results with
`output`, `tokens`, and `meta`.

```r
conn_s <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize")

# URL mode
req_s  <- new_summarizer_request(conn_s,
                                 url = "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSRHeXYDLko",
                                 engine = "muriel",
                                 summary_type = "summary",
                                 target_language = "EN")

res_s  <- summarizer_perform(req_s, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))
summarizer_output(res_s)
summarizer_tokens(res_s)
summarizer_meta(res_s)

# Text mode
req_s2 <- new_summarizer_request(conn_s,
                                 text = "Paste long text here ...",
                                 engine = "cecil",
                                 summary_type = "takeaway",
                                 target_language = "EN",
                                 cache = FALSE)

res_s2 <- summarizer_perform(req_s2)
summarizer_output(res_s2)
```

## Testing with vcr

Record once, replay thereafter. Be sure to filter your API key from cassettes.

```r
# tests/testthat/setup-vcr.R
vcr::vcr_configure(
  dir = testthat::test_path("fixtures/cassettes"),
  record = Sys.getenv("VCR_RECORD_MODE", unset = "once"),
  filter_sensitive_data = list("<<KAGI_API_KEY>>" = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY")),
  filter_request_headers = list("Authorization"),
  match_requests_on = c("method", "uri", "body")
)

# In tests:
vcr::use_cassette("search_basic", {
  conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search")
  res  <- kagi_search_once('openalex api')
  testthat::expect_s3_class(res, 'kagi_search_results')
})
```

## Error Handling

HTTP errors are converted to informative R errors using `httr2::req_error()`.
API-provided messages (if present) are preserved in thrown conditions. Consider
defining custom condition classes to allow `tryCatch()` by users.

## Rate Limits & Courtesy

Respect `Retry-After` headers and consider a simple backoff on 429/503 responses.
Avoid hammering endpoints; cache raw JSON on disk when feasible by using the
`path` argument of `*_perform()`.

## Session info

```r
sessionInfo()
```
